skill.name: eightctl
skill.description: Control Eight Sleep pods (status, temperature, alarms, schedules).
skill.summary: Control Eight Sleep pod features including status, temperature, alarms, and schedules through CLI commands.
skill.author: aiskillstore

security_audit.summary: Official skill from anthropics/skills repository. The skill executes the eightctl CLI tool to control Eight Sleep pods. Requires authentication via config file or environment variables. No prompt injection attempts detected. Safe to publish.

content.user_title: Control Eight Sleep Pods from CLI
content.value_statement: Managing Eight Sleep pod features through the web interface is slow and requires manual navigation. This skill enables direct CLI control over temperature, alarms, schedules, and bed status for faster smart home automation.
actual_capabilities: Check current Eight Sleep pod status and settings
Adjust bed temperature on both sides independently
Manage alarms with list, create, and dismiss commands
Control audio features including state, play, and pause
Retrieve base information and adjust base angle
limitations: Requires eightctl binary to be installed on the system
Needs authentication credentials in config file or environment variables
Uses unofficial API that may be rate-limited
Cannot control features not supported by eightctl CLI

use_cases.title: Automate bedtime temperature adjustments
use_cases.description: Set your Eight Sleep pod to cool down automatically at bedtime without opening the app

use_cases.title: Quick alarm management from terminal
use_cases.description: Create, view, or dismiss alarms directly from your command line workflow

use_cases.title: Schedule bed warming routines
use_cases.description: Configure heating schedules to pre-warm your bed before you wake up

prompt_templates.title: Check bed status
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the current status of my Eight Sleep pod
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick overview of current bed settings and state

prompt_templates.title: Adjust temperature
prompt_templates.prompt: Set my bed temperature to 18 degrees
prompt_templates.scenario: Change heating or cooling level for comfort

prompt_templates.title: Manage alarms
prompt_templates.prompt: List all my alarms and create a new one for 7am
prompt_templates.scenario: Review existing alarms and add new wake-up times

prompt_templates.title: View and update schedules
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my current heating schedule and update it to warm the bed at 6am
prompt_templates.scenario: Configure automated temperature changes throughout the day

output_examples.output: Current status: Bed is ON, left side temperature set to 20°C, right side at 19°C. Base angle: 45 degrees. Last synced: 2 minutes ago.
Alarm created successfully for 7:00 AM. Alarm ID: alarm_12345. You can dismiss it later with 'eightctl alarm dismiss alarm_12345'.
Temperature updated to 18°C on both sides. The bed will reach this temperature in approximately 15 minutes.

best_practices: Store credentials in the config file rather than environment variables for persistent authentication
Avoid repeated login attempts to prevent hitting API rate limits
Confirm temperature changes before executing to avoid discomfort
Test commands with status checks first to verify authentication is working
anti_patterns: Do not run authentication commands in rapid succession as this may trigger rate limiting
Avoid setting extreme temperatures without testing gradual adjustments first
Do not rely on this skill for critical alarm management without backup reminders
Avoid dismissing alarms without confirming which specific alarm you are targeting

faq.question: How do I authenticate with my Eight Sleep account?
faq.answer: Store your credentials in ~/.config/eightctl/config.yaml or set the EIGHTCTL_EMAIL and EIGHTCTL_PASSWORD environment variables before running commands.

faq.question: Can I control each side of the bed independently?
faq.answer: Yes, eightctl supports controlling left and right sides separately. Specify the side when adjusting temperature or checking status.

faq.question: What happens if the API rate limit is reached?
faq.answer: The unofficial API has rate limits. If exceeded, wait a few minutes before retrying. Avoid repeated login attempts to prevent hitting limits.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all Eight Sleep pod models?
faq.answer: This skill works with any model supported by the eightctl CLI tool. Check the eightctl documentation for specific model compatibility.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with other smart home automations?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use this skill within scripts or automation workflows that support Claude Code to create complex bedtime routines.

faq.question: Is my password stored securely?
faq.answer: Credentials are stored in your local config file or environment variables. The skill does not handle password storage directly; it relies on eightctl's authentication mechanism.
